news_items.title: South Africa vs South Korea team news and predicted lineups
news_items.text:
# South Africa vs South Korea team news and predicted lineups

Published: 2026-06-22T21:13:04+00:00
Source: squawka.com (squawka.com)
Language: en

## Story

South Korea will be hoping to book their spot in the 2026 World Cup knockout stages when they face South Africa on Thursday at 2am (UK time).

South Korea are in a good position going into the final round of Group A fixtures. They only need a point to secure their place in the last-32, though top spot cannot be reached. South Korea opened the World Cup with a 2-1 win over Czech Republic, but fell to a 1-0 defeat to Mexico — which means they cannot overtake the co-hosts.

Meanwhile, South Africa know they have it all to do to reach the knockout stages. Bafana Bafana have taken just one point from their opening two games. They started the tournament with a defeat in the opening match, losing to co-hosts Mexico. They looked on course for another defeat last time out, but did salvage a point against Czech Republic thanks to a late penalty. South Africa need to beat South Korea and hope Czech Republic drop points against Mexico to guarantee second. But a win should be enough for a knockout stage, regardless. Anything else, though,

South Korea opened the World Cup with a 2-1 win over Czech Republic, but fell to a 1-0 defeat to Mexico — which means they cannot overtake the co-hosts.

Meanwhile, South Africa know they have it all to do to reach the knockout stages. Bafana Bafana have taken just one point from their opening two games. South Africa need to beat South Korea and hope Czech Republic drop points against Mexico to guarantee second. But a win should be enough for a knockout stage, regardless. Anything else, though,

news_items.title: South Africa vs. South Korea  at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN
news_items.author: Sunaadh Sagar
news_items.text:
South Africa vs. South Korea at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

South Africa and South Korea face off in their final Group A clash in the 2026 World Cup at the Estadio Monterrey on Thursday.

South Africa come into this game needing a win, after a hard-fought 1-1 draw against Czechia kept them alive in the World Cup following their 0-2 loss to Mexico in the opener.

South Korea followed their opening 2-1 win over Czechia with a narrow 0-1 loss to Mexico and will fancy their chances of qualification.

The two teams are facing each other for the first time.

Here's everything you need to know about Thursday's game.

## How to watch:

The match will be available on BBC Two in the UK, Fox Sports in the U.S., Zee5 in India and SBS in Australia. You can also follow ESPN's live updates.

## Key Details:

Date, kick-off time: U.S. ET: Wednesday June 24, 9 p.m. UK BST: Thursday June 25, 2 a.m. India IST: Thursday June 25, 6.30 a.m. Australia AEST: Thursday June 25, 11 a.m.

Venue: Estadio BBVA, Guadalupe, Mexico Referee: Facundo Tello (Argentina)

## Team News and Predict

# Hong Myung-bo's "Lee Kang-in Excluded from Starting Lineup" Bold Move, "Draw Secures 32nd Place" Against South Africa: "2-3 Changes" Expected

Published:2026.06.24 ・ 13:31

Kim Myeongseok

Add as preferred source

*This content was translated by AI.

The 2026 CONCACAF World Cup Group A qualifier between South Korea and Mexico was held on the 19th (Korea time) at Estadio Akron in Guadalajara, Mexico. Lee Kang-in is seen concluding the match, which ended in a 0-1 loss to Mexico. 2026.06.19. /Photo=Chief correspondent Kim Jin-kyung

The "day of decisive battle" is one day away. This is the final group stage match for the South Korean national football team to determine whether they will advance to the 2026 FIFA North, Central America and Caribbean World Cup round of 32. A favorable situation exists where simply avoiding defeat allows South Korea to advance to the round of 32 on their own. Coach Hong Myung-bo's final dilemma regarding the selection of the starting lineup is also deepening.

news_items.title: 'We will win' — South Korea stakes World Cup round-of-32 fate on South Africa clash - The Herald Business
news_items.author: Chough Yong-jik
news_items.text:
'We will win' — South Korea stakes World Cup round-of-32 fate on South Africa clash - The Herald Business

# 'We will win' — South Korea stakes World Cup round-of-32 fate on South Africa clash

by Chough Yong-jik

View Korean Original

Link copied!

Hong signals two or three lineup changes for Thursday's Group A finale; Son's role, Castrop's debut among key questions; yellow-card management also in focus

South Korea's Lee Kang-in trains with teammates at Estadio Universitario in Monterrey, Mexico, on Wednesday ahead of the team's Group A finale against South Africa at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. [Yonhap]

"If we start thinking a draw is enough, we could find ourselves in trouble. Our opponent is tricky. We will prepare for this match with the mindset that we must win."

South Korea head coach Hong Myung-bo struck a determined tone at a press conference held Wednesday at Estadio Universitario in Monterrey, Mexico. "Looking back at our World Cup history, we have often found ourselves in situations where we had to win to advance," he said. "The current situation — where a draw would still leave us second in the group — is not bad, but it is not particularly helpful either."

South Korea

## Story

South Korea will be hoping to book their spot in the 2026 World Cup knockout stages when they face South Africa on Thursday at 2am (UK time).

South Korea are in a good position going into the final round of Group A fixtures. They only need a point to secure their place in the last-32, though top spot cannot be reached. South Korea opened the World Cup with a 2-1 win over Czech Republic, but fell to a 1-0 defeat to Mexico — which means they cannot overtake the co-hosts.

Meanwhile, South Africa know they have it all to do to reach the knockout stages. Bafana Bafana have taken just one point from their opening two games. They started the tournament with a defeat in the opening match, losing to co-hosts Mexico. They looked on course for another defeat last time out, but did salvage a point against Czech Republic thanks to a late penalty. play

South Africa and South Korea face off in their final Group A clash in the 2026 World Cup at the Estadio Monterrey on Thursday.

South Africa come into this game needing a win, after a hard-fought 1-1 draw against Czechia kept them alive in the World Cup following their 0-2 loss to Mexico in the opener.

South Korea followed their opening 2-1 win over Czechia with a narrow 0-1 loss to Mexico and will fancy their chances of qualification.

The two teams are facing each other for the first time.

Here's everything you need to know about Thursday's game.

## How to watch:

The match will be available on BBC Two in the UK, Fox Sports in the U.S., Zee5 in India and SBS in Australia. You can also follow ESPN's live updates.

## Key Details:

Date, kick-off time: U.S. ET: Wednesday June 24, 9 p.m. UK BST: Thursday June 25, 2 a.m. India IST: Thursday June 25, 6.30 a.m. Australia AEST: Thursday June 25, 11 a.m.

Venue: Estadio BBVA, Guadalupe, Mexico Referee: Facundo Tello (Argentina)
